name: ConvertTo-Html

options.name: -As
options.description: Determines whether the object is formatted as a table or a list. Valid values are Table and List . The default value is Table .

options.name: -Body
options.description: Specifies the text to add after the opening `<BODY>` tag. By default, there is no text in that position.

options.name: -Charset
options.description: Specifies text to add to the opening `<charset>` tag. By default, there is no text in that position.

name: -CssUri
-cu
-uri
options.description: Specifies the Uniform Resource Identifier (URI) of the cascading style sheet (CSS) that is applied to the HTML file. The URI is included in a style sheet link in the output.

options.name: -Fragment
options.description: Generates only an HTML table. The `<HTML>`, `<HEAD>`, `<TITLE>`, and `<BODY>` tags are omitted.

options.name: -Head
options.description: Specifies the content of the `<HEAD>` tag. The default is `<title>HTML TABLE</title>`. If you use the Head parameter, the Title parameter is ignored.

options.name: -InputObject
options.description: Specifies the objects to be represented in HTML. Enter a variable that contains the objects or type a command or expression that gets the objects.

options.name: -Meta
options.description: Specifies text to add to the opening `<meta>` tag. By default, there is no text in that position.

options.name: -PostContent
options.description: Specifies text to add after the closing `` tag. By default, there is no text in that position.

options.name: -PreContent
options.description: Specifies text to add before the opening `<TABLE>` tag. By default, there is no text in that position.

options.name: -Property
options.description: Includes the specified properties of the objects in the HTML. The value of the Property parameter can be a new calculated property. The calculated property can be a script block or a hash table. Valid key-value pairs are:

options.name: -Title
options.description: Specifies a title for the HTML file, that is, the text that appears between the `<TITLE>` tags.

options.name: -Transitional
options.description: Changes the `DOCTYPE` to XHTML Transitional DTD , Default `DOCTYPE` is XHTML Strict DTD .

name: -Debug
-db
options.description: Displays programmer-level detail about the operation done by the command. This parameter works only when the command generates a debugging message.

name: -ErrorAction
-ea
options.description: Determines how the cmdlet responds to a non-terminating error from the command. This parameter works only when the command generates a non-terminating error.

name: -ErrorVariable
-ev
options.description: Error records are automatically stored in the `$Error` automatic variable. When you use the ErrorVariable parameter on a command, PowerShell also stores the error records emitted by the command in the variable specified by the parameter.

name: -InformationAction
-infa
options.description: Within the command or script in which it's used, the InformationAction common parameter overrides the value of the `$InformationPreference` preference variable, which by default is set to SilentlyContinue.

name: -InformationVariable
-iv
options.description: When you use the InformationVariable common parameter, information records are stored in the variable specified by the parameter.

name: -OutBuffer
-ob
options.description: Determines the number of objects to accumulate in a buffer before any objects are sent through the pipeline. If you omit this parameter, objects are sent as they're generated.

name: -OutVariable
-ov
options.description: Stores output objects from the command in the specified variable in addition to sending the output along the pipeline.

name: -PipelineVariable
-pv
options.description: PipelineVariable allows access to the most recent value passed into the next pipeline segment by the command that uses this parameter. Any command in the pipeline can access the value using the named PipelineVariable. The value is assigned to the variable when it's passed into the next pipeline segment.

name: -ProgressAction
-proga
options.description: Determines how PowerShell responds to progress updates generated by a script, cmdlet, or provider, such as the progress bars generated by the Write-Progress cmdlet. The Write-Progress cmdlet creates progress bars that show a command's status.

name: -Verbose
-vb
options.description: Displays detailed information about the operation done by the command. This information resembles the information in a trace or in a transaction log. This parameter works only when the command generates a verbose message.

name: -WarningAction
-wa
options.description: Determines how the cmdlet responds to a warning from the command. Continue is the default value. This parameter works only when the command generates a warning message.

name: -WarningVariable
-wv
options.description: Stores warning records about the command in the specified variable.

args.name: -Property
args.description: Includes the specified properties of the objects in the HTML. The value of the Property parameter can be a new calculated property. The calculated property can be a script block or a hash table. Valid key-value pairs are:

args.name: -Head
args.description: Specifies the content of the `<HEAD>` tag. The default is `<title>HTML TABLE</title>`. If you use the Head parameter, the Title parameter is ignored.

args.name: -Title
args.description: Specifies a title for the HTML file, that is, the text that appears between the `<TITLE>` tags.

args.name: -Body
args.description: Specifies the text to add after the opening `<BODY>` tag. By default, there is no text in that position.

description: Converts .NET objects into HTML that can be displayed in a Web browser.
